hard_rules: temporary_scaffold entries require scaffold_reason, expires_after, migration_target, and delete_when
temporary_monitor entries require scaffold_reason, success_criteria, migration_target, retirement_action, delete_when, and target_runtime_owner
runtime_crutch entries require weakness_indicator, target_runtime_owner, and migration_target
one_time_closure_guard entries require expires_after, migration_target, and delete_when
self_enforcement_validator entries require runtime_enforced_by and validation_scope

entries.invariant: Web retrieval reliability evidence is wired into release proof packs and profile gates.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: kernel/web_conduit + kernel/batch_query
entries.scaffold_reason: Legacy V11 web retrieval TODO/SRS rows needed aggregate closure proof after implementation was already split across web conduit, batch query, and web tooling artifacts.
entries.migration_target: Merge covered checks into ops:web-tooling:reliability:guard and ops:web-conduit:openclaw-media:closure:guard once V11 rows are archived.
entries.delete_when: Permanent web tooling and web conduit guards cover scoped search, provider fallback, fetch resilience, and batch-query synthesis quality without referencing legacy closure rows.
entries.strengthen_signal: If this remains needed, kernel web retrieval contracts are too dispersed and should be consolidated into a permanent web retrieval runtime proof artifact.

entries.invariant: Installer offline/cache, wrapper repair, release-tag refresh, Windows parity, and summary truth contracts remain release evidence.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: install.sh + install.ps1 + infring doctor/verify-install
entries.validation_scope: Cross-shell installer contracts and release-governance proof-pack wiring.
entries.strengthen_signal: If installer failures recur, move more summary/wrapper verification into infring doctor so CI only verifies the authoritative runtime diagnosis.

entries.invariant: Web evidence extraction must reject chrome, scaffold leakage, low-attribution text, and provider/cache contract drift.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: kernel/web_conduit extraction and batch-query synthesis contracts
entries.validation_scope: Fixture-backed web extraction and soak-derived reliability artifact.
entries.strengthen_signal: Any new sanitizer-only rule should be reviewed for migration into kernel extraction policy instead of living only in tests.

entries.invariant: Workspace/file tooling routes must use workspace probes and never misdirect into web/tool fallback paths.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: orchestration typed probes + workspace tooling contracts
entries.validation_scope: Workspace read/search, repo path targeting, and mixed workspace/web routing soak.
entries.strengthen_signal: If this needs more scaffolding, orchestration probe envelopes are still too weak for file tooling.

entries.invariant: Tool routing must be probe-driven and return specific missing-probe reasons instead of heuristic fallback.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: surface/orchestration planner preconditions and ingress classifier
entries.strengthen_signal: Any heuristic pass here means routing authority belongs deeper in orchestration, not in repair tests.

entries.invariant: Shell, orchestration, gateways, and kernel must not import forbidden authority surfaces across layer boundaries.
entries.strengthen_signal: Repeated violations indicate missing module ownership APIs or nexuses, not just bad imports.

entries.invariant: Shell must remain presentation/collection only and avoid health inference, retry authority, lane selection, and policy branching.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: kernel freshness enums and backend-provided runtime state
entries.validation_scope: Shell truth-leak scan and freshness contract checks.
entries.strengthen_signal: Any persistent finding means the backend is not exposing enough authoritative state for the shell to stay thin.

entries.invariant: Local/session/generated churn must not pollute root or release-critical surfaces.
entries.drift_surface: Workspace root, generated reports, session outputs, and shadow-agent churn
entries.validation_scope: Repository status and root-output hygiene scan.
entries.strengthen_signal: Repeated churn findings mean producers should be moved under local/state or local/workspace/shadow, not merely ignored.

entries.invariant: Planner/runtime/gateway/workflow orchestration quality artifacts must stay required release evidence.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: surface/orchestration quality policy and runtime workflow contracts
entries.strengthen_signal: If this gate catches repeated drift, individual orchestration guards should become runtime receipt checks.

entries.invariant: Release profile, proof-track, gateway chaos, installer evidence, and proof-pack policy must agree before release readiness.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: release-gates policy and proof-pack assembly
entries.strengthen_signal: If this remains the only protection for a release invariant, move that invariant into release_proof_pack_assemble or runtime proof gates.

entries.invariant: Memory continuity closure evidence covers recent-floor, full-history recall, and sleep cleanup before archival.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: memory continuity runtime and sleep cleanup policies
entries.scaffold_reason: Backlog closure needed a focused evidence bundle after memory continuity implementation was spread across runtime and policy surfaces.
entries.migration_target: Merge remaining checks into permanent memory durability and continuity gates.
entries.delete_when: Permanent memory durability/continuity gates cover the same invariants without closure-row lineage.
entries.strengthen_signal: If retained, memory continuity needs a single authoritative runtime health artifact.

entries.invariant: Knowledge graph acceleration closure evidence remains wired while assimilation rows are burned down.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: kernel knowledge graph query acceleration path
entries.scaffold_reason: Assimilation closure needed proof that acceleration artifacts and policies were wired before archiving rows.
entries.migration_target: Merge into a permanent knowledge graph query performance/reliability gate.
entries.delete_when: A permanent query acceleration gate validates latency, correctness, and proof-pack evidence directly.
entries.strengthen_signal: If retained, query acceleration lacks a stable runtime performance contract.

entries.invariant: OpenClaw media/provider web-conduit imports are evidenced and release-visible during assimilation cleanup.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: kernel/web_conduit media/provider runtime
entries.scaffold_reason: Assimilation closure needed aggregate proof across media, fetch, image, and PDF runtime slices.
entries.migration_target: Merge into permanent web media/provider reliability gates.
entries.delete_when: Permanent web media/provider gates cover the imported runtime behavior without assimilation lineage.
entries.strengthen_signal: If retained, web media capability needs better first-class runtime proof.

entries.invariant: Manual assimilation support policy and ledgers remain coherent while the assimilation process is being hardened.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: orchestration assimilation policy + workspace assimilation ledgers
entries.scaffold_reason: Assimilation is still partly manual, so a support guard tracks process evidence until runtime assimilation authority owns the flow.
entries.migration_target: Move durable checks into Assimilation Map/Priority Ledger/Active Queue runtime policy and the assimilation authority guard.
entries.delete_when: Assimilation authority runtime enforces ledger burn-down, decisions, and workflow/raw-capability separation without a closure support guard.
entries.strengthen_signal: If retained, assimilation needs stronger orchestration-owned runtime authority instead of manual ledger policing.

entries.invariant: Shell/client deauthority work remains evidenced while client compatibility names are retired.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: backend freshness enums + shell truth-leak guard
entries.scaffold_reason: Client deauthority closure needed a focused bridge while old client surfaces and new shell terminology coexisted.
entries.migration_target: Merge into ops:shell:truth-leak:guard and shell transition tracker.
entries.delete_when: Shell truth-leak guard and shell transition tracker cover deauthority invariants without closure lineage.
entries.strengthen_signal: If retained, backend state projection is still insufficiently authoritative for shell code to stay truth-free.

entries.invariant: Developer-facing public facade naming and command contracts remain coherent during transition.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: CLI command registry and public facade contracts
entries.scaffold_reason: DX facade cleanup needed temporary aggregate proof while public/canonical terms were converging.
entries.migration_target: Merge stable checks into command-registry and terminology transition guards.
entries.delete_when: Command registry and terminology inventory enforce public facade contracts directly.
entries.strengthen_signal: If retained, public CLI/runtime facade contracts are too diffuse.

entries.invariant: Gemini feedback-derived hardening remains evidenced while feedback intake rows are retired.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: workspace/tooling/eval guards produced from feedback intake
entries.scaffold_reason: External feedback produced a batch of cross-cutting TODO items that needed temporary closure evidence.
entries.migration_target: Merge recurring feedback checks into eval issue synthesis and quality gates.
entries.delete_when: Eval issue synthesis can ingest, classify, and enforce feedback without a named Gemini closure guard.
entries.strengthen_signal: If retained, eval feedback ingestion is not yet strong enough to absorb external critique automatically.

entries.invariant: Incident governance policy, runbooks, gates, CI, profiles, and proof-pack evidence remain aligned.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: incident governance policy + release proof-pack assembly
entries.strengthen_signal: If this gate repeatedly catches drift, incident state should move into a stronger runtime incident ledger.

entries.invariant: Production closure evidence is complete before release readiness is claimed.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: release closure board + production readiness gates
entries.strengthen_signal: If this gate remains broad, split weak buckets into runtime-owned gates instead of one production umbrella.

entries.invariant: Runtime closure board remains synchronized with release-blocking runtime buckets.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: runtime closure board policy + proof-pack assembly
entries.validation_scope: Runtime closure board completeness and release-blocker visibility.
entries.strengthen_signal: If board-only enforcement persists, runtime subsystems need more direct pass/fail artifacts.

entries.invariant: New visible capabilities align with runtime closure buckets and proof burden.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: capability proof burden guard + runtime closure feature alignment policy
entries.strengthen_signal: If this gate catches repeated feature drift, PR/runtime admission should require proof burden metadata earlier.

entries.invariant: Tooling and Task Fabric closure evidence covers typed probes, route misdirection, resident-first transport, workspace/web evidence, and proof-carrying mutations during consolidation.
entries.runtime_enforced_by: orchestration typed probes + task fabric mutation contracts
entries.scaffold_reason: Task Fabric closure spans multiple permanent gates and needed temporary aggregate evidence while the feature stabilized.
entries.migration_target: Merge into typed probe, tool-route, workspace/web tooling, and task fabric mutation permanent gates.
entries.delete_when: Permanent gates cover each Task Fabric invariant without an aggregate closure guard.
entries.strengthen_signal: If retained, Task Fabric needs a single runtime authority artifact for mutation and route proof.
